content.problemStatement: Sereja have permutation **p[1], p[2], ..., p[n]**. Sereja have made **n** permutations **q[1], q[2], ..., q[n]**, **q[i]** is permutation **p** without element **i** (we subtract 1 from all elements bigger than **i**). For example **8 1 2 3 4 5 6 7** without **5** is **7 1 2 3 4 5 6**. Sereja have made random shuffle of **q**. Help Sereja find initial permutation **p**.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with an integer n, the length of the permutation. Then n lines follow, each containing a permutation of length n-1.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the original permutation p of length n.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The original permutation p is [1, 2, 3] because the missing element in each of the given permutations q[i] is the i-th element of p.
The original permutation p is [1, 2, 3, 4] because the missing element in each of the given permutations q[i] is the i-th element of p.
The original permutation p is [1, 1, 1, 1, 1] because the missing element in each of the given permutations q[i] is the i-th element of p.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by comparing each permutation q[i] with q[(i+1)%n] to find the missing element, which is the i-th element of the original permutation p. This approach works because each permutation q[i] is the original permutation p without the i-th element.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * n^2) per test case, O(T * n^2) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n^2) — for storing the permutations q.
